chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Here, we tackle backpropagation, the core algorithm behind how neural networks learn.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: After a quick recap for where we are, the first thing I'll do is an intuitive walkthrough

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: for what the algorithm is actually doing, without any reference to the formulas.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Then, for those of you who do want to dive into the math, the next video goes into the

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: calculus underlying all this.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: If you watched the last two videos, or if you're just jumping in with the appropriate

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: background, you know what a neural network is, and how it feeds forward information.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Here we're doing the classic example of recognizing handwritten digits, whose pixel values get fed

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: into the first layer of the network with 784 neurons, and I've been showing a network with

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: two hidden layers having just 16 neurons each, and an output layer of 10 neurons, indicating

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: which digit the network is choosing as its answer.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: I'm also expecting you to understand gradient descent, as described in the last video, and

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: how what we mean by learning is that we want to find which weights and biases minimize a

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: certain cost function.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: As a quick reminder, for the cost of a single training example, what you do is take the output

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: that the network gives, along with the output that you wanted it to give, and you just add

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: up the squares of the differences between each component.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Doing this for all of your tens of thousands of training examples, and averaging the results,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: this gives you the total cost of the network.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: As if that's not enough to think about, as described in the last video, the thing we're

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: looking for is the negative gradient of this cost function, which tells you how you need

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: to change all of the weights and biases, all of these connections, so as to most efficiently

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: decrease the cost.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Backpropagation, the topic of this video, is an algorithm for computing that crazy complicated

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: gradient.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: And the one idea from the last video that I really want you to hold firmly in your mind

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: right now, is that because thinking of the gradient vector as a direction in 13,000 dimensions

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: is, to put it lightly, beyond the scope of our imaginations, there's another way you can

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: think about it.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: The magnitude of each component here is telling you how sensitive the cost function is to each

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: weight and bias.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: For example, let's say you go through the process I'm about to describe, and you compute

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: the negative gradient, and the component associated with the weight on this edge here comes out

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: to be 3.2, while the component associated with this edge here comes out as 0.1.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: The way you would interpret that is that the cost of the function is 32 times more sensitive

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: to changes in that first weight.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So if you were to wiggle that value just a little bit, it's going to cause some change

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: to the cost, and that change is 32 times greater than what the same wiggle to that second weight

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: would give.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Personally, when I was first learning about backpropagation, I think the most confusing

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: aspect was just the notation and the index chasing of it all.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: But once you unwrap what each part of this algorithm is really doing, each individual effect that

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: it's having is actually pretty intuitive.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: It's just that there's a lot of little adjustments getting layered on top of each other.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So I'm going to start things off here with a complete disregard for the notation, and just

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: step through those effects that each training example is having on the weights and biases.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Because the cost function involves averaging a certain cost per example over all the tens

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: of thousands of training examples, the way that we adjust the weights and biases for a

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: single gradient descent step also depends on every single example.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Or rather, in principle it should, but for computational efficiency we're going to do a little trick

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: later to keep you from needing to hit every single example for every single step.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: In either case, right now, all we're going to do is focus our attention on one single example,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: this image of a 2.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: What effect should this one training example have on how the weights and biases get adjusted?

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Let's say we're at a point where the network is not well trained yet, so the activations

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: in the output are going to look pretty random, maybe something like 0.5, 0.8, 0.2, on and on.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: We can't directly change those activations, we only have influence on the weights and biases.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: But it is helpful to keep track of which adjustments we wish should take place to that output layer.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: And since we want it to classify the image as a 2, we want that third value to get nudged

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: up, while all of the others get nudged down.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Moreover, the sizes of these nudges should be proportional to how far away each current

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: value is from its target value.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: For example, the increase to that number 2 neuron's activation is in a sense more important

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: than the decrease to the number 8 neuron, which is already pretty close to where it should be.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So zooming in further, let's focus just on this one neuron, the one whose activation we

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: wish to increase.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Remember, that activation is defined as a certain weighted sum of all of the activations in the

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: previous layer, plus a bias, which is all then plugged into something like the sigmoid squishification

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: function, or a ReLU.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So there are three different avenues that can team up together to help increase that activation.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: You can increase the bias, you can increase the weights, and you can change the activations

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: from the previous layer.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Focusing just on how the weights should be adjusted, notice how the weights actually have

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: differing levels of influence.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: The connections with the brightest neurons from the preceding layer have the biggest effect,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: since those weights are multiplied by larger activation values.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So if you were to increase one of those weights, it actually has a stronger influence on the ultimate

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: cost function than increasing the weights of connections with dimmer neurons, at least as far

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: as this one training example is concerned.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Remember, when we talk about gradient descent, we don't just care about whether each component

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: should get nudged up or down, we care about which ones give you the most bang for your

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: butt.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: This, by the way, is at least somewhat reminiscent of a theory in neuroscience for how biological

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: networks of neurons learn, Hebbian theory, often summed up in the phrase, neurons that

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: fire together wire together.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Here, the biggest increases to weights, the biggest strengthening of connections, happens

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: between neurons which are the most active, and the ones which we wish to become more active.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: In a sense, the neurons that are firing while seeing a two get more strongly linked to those

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: firing when thinking about a two.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: To be clear, I really am not in a position to make statements one way or another about whether

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: artificial networks of neurons behave anything like biological brains, and this fires together,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: together idea comes with a couple meaningful asterisks, but taken as a very loose analogy,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: I do find it interesting to note.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Anyway, the third way that we can help increase this neuron's activation is by changing all

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: the activations in the previous layer.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Namely, if everything connected to that digit 2 neuron with a positive weight got brighter,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: and if everything connected with a negative weight got dimmer, then that digit 2 neuron would

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: become more active.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: And similar to the weight changes, you're going to get the most bang for your buck by seeking

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: changes that are proportional to the size of the corresponding weights.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Now of course, we cannot directly influence those activations, we only have control over

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: the weights and biases.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: But just as with the last layer, it's helpful to just keep a note of what those desired changes

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: are.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So keep in mind, zooming out one step here, this is only what that digit 2 output neuron

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: wants.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Remember, we also want all of the other neurons in the last layer to become less active, and

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: each of those other output neurons has its own thoughts about what should happen to that

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: second to last layer.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So the desire of this digit 2 neuron is added together with the desires of all the other

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: output neurons for what should happen to this second to last layer, again in proportion to

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: the corresponding weights, AND in proportion to how much each of those neurons needs to change.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: This right here is where the idea of propagating backwards comes in.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: By adding together all these desired effects, you basically get a list of nudges that you want

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: to happen to this second to last layer.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: And once you have those, you can recursively apply the same process to the relevant weights

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: and biases that determine those values, repeating the same process I just walked through and

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: moving backwards through the network.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: And zooming out a bit further, remember that this is all just how a single training example

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: wishes to nudge each one of those weights and biases.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: If we only listened to what that 2 wanted, the network would ultimately be incentivized

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: just to classify all images as a 2.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So what you do is go through this same backprop routine for every other training example, recording

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: how each of them would like to change the weights and biases, and you average together

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: those desired changes.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: This collection here, of the averaged nudges to each weight and bias, is, loosely speaking,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: the negative gradient of the cost function referenced in the last video, or at least something

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: proportional to it.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: I say loosely speaking only because I have yet to get quantitatively precise about those nudges,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: but if you understood every change that I just referenced, why some are proportionally bigger

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: than others, and how they all need to be added together, you understand the mechanics for

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: what backpropagation is actually doing.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: By the way, in practice, it takes computers an extremely long time to add up the influence

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: of every training example, every gradient descent step.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So here's what's commonly done instead.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: You randomly shuffle your training data, and then divide it into a whole bunch of mini-batches,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: let's say each one having 100 training examples.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Then you compute a step according to the mini-batch.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: It's not going to be the actual gradient of the cost function, which depends on all of the

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: training data, not this tiny subset.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So it's not the most efficient step downhill.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: But each mini-batch does give you a pretty good approximation, and more importantly,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: it gives you a significant computational speedup.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: If you were to plot the trajectory of your network under the relevant cost surface, it

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: would be a little more like a drunk man stumbling aimlessly down a hill, but taking quick steps,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: rather than a carefully calculating man determining the exact downhill direction of each step before

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: taking a very slow and careful step in that direction.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: This technique is referred to as stochastic gradient descent.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: There's a lot going on here, so let's just sum it up for ourselves, shall we?

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Backpropagation is the algorithm for determining how a single training example would like to

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: nudge the weights and biases, not just in terms of whether they should go up or down, but in

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: terms of what relative proportions to those changes cause the most rapid decrease to the cost.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: A true gradient descent step would involve doing this for all your tens and thousands

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: of training examples and averaging the desired changes that you get.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: But that's computationally slow, so instead you randomly subdivide the data into these mini-batches,

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: and compute each step with respect to a mini-batch.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Repeatedly going through all of the mini-batches and making these adjustments, you will converge

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: towards a local minimum of the cost function.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Which is to say, your network is going to end up doing a really good job on the training examples.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So with all of that said, every line of code that would go into implementing backprop actually

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: corresponds with something that you have now seen, at least in informal terms.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: But sometimes knowing what the math does is only half the battle, and just representing

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: the damn thing is where it gets all muddled and confusing.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So, for those of you who do want to go deeper, the next video goes through the same ideas

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: that were just presented here, but in terms of the underlying calculus, which should hopefully

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: make it a little more familiar as you see the topic in other resources.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: Before that, one thing worth emphasizing is that for this algorithm to work, and this goes

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: for all sorts of machine learning beyond just neural networks, you need a lot of training

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: data.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: In our case, one thing that makes handwritten digits such a nice example is that there exists

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: the MNIST database, with so many examples that have been labeled by humans.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So a common challenge that those of you working in machine learning will be familiar with is

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: just getting the labeled training data that you actually need, whether that's having people

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: label tens of thousands of images, or whatever other data type you might be dealing with.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So a common challenge that you have used to learn in the birazdevil Fear Lossabe ...

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: I'm going to watch it online!

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: I'm going to watch my channel when you would search for the aim in muitas

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: times.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: So I know for you, if you don't find your own programming, but I would do it a bit less

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: appearance in any problem.

chunks.text: ання platform.

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: However, you will find yourself already in two levels przed- positioneded

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: safety einesuph physically on the ground and you have the option in a lot of

chunks.title: Backpropagation, intuitively Deep Learning
chunks.text: how to I have hundreds of Damn- estable or々 Spaceätte� його

text: Here, we tackle backpropagation, the core algorithm behind how neural networks learn. After a quick recap for where we are, the first thing I'll do is an intuitive walkthrough for what the algorithm is actually doing, without any reference to the formulas. Then, for those of you who do want to dive into the math, the next video goes into the calculus underlying all this. If you watched the last two videos, or if you're just jumping in with the appropriate background, you know what a neural network is, and how it feeds forward information. Here we're doing the classic example of recognizing handwritten digits, whose pixel values get fed into the first layer of the network with 784 neurons, and I've been showing a network with two hidden layers having just 16 neurons each, and an output layer of 10 neurons, indicating which digit the network is choosing as its answer. I'm also expecting you to understand gradient descent, as described in the last video, and how what we mean by learning is that we want to find which weights and biases minimize a certain cost function. As a quick reminder, for the cost of a single training example, what you do is take the output that the network gives, along with the output that you wanted it to give, and you just add up the squares of the differences between each component. Doing this for all of your tens of thousands of training examples, and averaging the results, this gives you the total cost of the network. As if that's not enough to think about, as described in the last video, the thing we're looking for is the negative gradient of this cost function, which tells you how you need to change all of the weights and biases, all of these connections, so as to most efficiently decrease the cost. Backpropagation, the topic of this video, is an algorithm for computing that crazy complicated gradient. And the one idea from the last video that I really want you to hold firmly in your mind right now, is that because thinking of the gradient vector as a direction in 13,000 dimensions is, to put it lightly, beyond the scope of our imaginations, there's another way you can think about it. The magnitude of each component here is telling you how sensitive the cost function is to each weight and bias. For example, let's say you go through the process I'm about to describe, and you compute the negative gradient, and the component associated with the weight on this edge here comes out to be 3.2, while the component associated with this edge here comes out as 0.1. The way you would interpret that is that the cost of the function is 32 times more sensitive to changes in that first weight. So if you were to wiggle that value just a little bit, it's going to cause some change to the cost, and that change is 32 times greater than what the same wiggle to that second weight would give. Personally, when I was first learning about backpropagation, I think the most confusing aspect was just the notation and the index chasing of it all. But once you unwrap what each part of this algorithm is really doing, each individual effect that it's having is actually pretty intuitive. It's just that there's a lot of little adjustments getting layered on top of each other. So I'm going to start things off here with a complete disregard for the notation, and just step through those effects that each training example is having on the weights and biases. Because the cost function involves averaging a certain cost per example over all the tens of thousands of training examples, the way that we adjust the weights and biases for a single gradient descent step also depends on every single example. Or rather, in principle it should, but for computational efficiency we're going to do a little trick later to keep you from needing to hit every single example for every single step. In either case, right now, all we're going to do is focus our attention on one single example, this image of a 2. What effect should this one training example have on how the weights and biases get adjusted? Let's say we're at a point where the network is not well trained yet, so the activations in the output are going to look pretty random, maybe something like 0.5, 0.8, 0.2, on and on. We can't directly change those activations, we only have influence on the weights and biases. But it is helpful to keep track of which adjustments we wish should take place to that output layer. And since we want it to classify the image as a 2, we want that third value to get nudged up, while all of the others get nudged down. Moreover, the sizes of these nudges should be proportional to how far away each current value is from its target value. For example, the increase to that number 2 neuron's activation is in a sense more important than the decrease to the number 8 neuron, which is already pretty close to where it should be. So zooming in further, let's focus just on this one neuron, the one whose activation we wish to increase. Remember, that activation is defined as a certain weighted sum of all of the activations in the previous layer, plus a bias, which is all then plugged into something like the sigmoid squishification function, or a ReLU. So there are three different avenues that can team up together to help increase that activation. You can increase the bias, you can increase the weights, and you can change the activations from the previous layer.
